resources.string: Allows the application to interact with the connectivity assistant module to detect and help users troubleshoot network connectivity issues.
You might try to connect to a WiFi, if your mobile network is slow.
You might try to contact your mobile operator and discuss to unblock your data.
Try toggling Airplane mode on and off.
Try switching off WiFi from settings and enable mobile data connection.
Disable WiFi to use your mobile data connection
Try logging into your wifWiFii connection.
Try moving closer to the cellular antenna.
Try moving closer to the WiFi router you are connected to.
Try to setup/switch to an eSim instead.
You might try to connect to a WiFi, if your mobile network is blocked by your mobile operator.
You might try to enable mobile data if it is disabled.
Can\'t refresh map. Did you enable Location and grant Location permissions?
%1$s won\'t work unless you enable Google Play services.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are missing from your device.
%1$s is having trouble with Google Play services. Please try again.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are not supported by your device.
%1$s won\'t run unless you update Google Play services.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are currently updating.
New version of Google Play services needed. It will update itself shortly.
This points you towards the connected cell tower.\n\nWalking in this direction, or out of the way of any obstacles may improve your signal.
You are connected to a 2G network, which may slow down apps and affect your experience.
You are connected to the mobile network, but there is a problem with your internet connection.
You are connected to WiFi, but there is a problem with your internet connection.
You are connected to a WiFi network that requires you to log in in order to access the internet.
There was an error running the connection test. Please try again.
Your internet connection appears to be slower than usual, and that may affect your internet experience.
Your mobile connection is slow and that may affect your internet experience.
Your phone does not have a mobile connection because the Airplane mode is on. Please switch the Airplane mode off to get back online.
Looks like your mobile data connection is disabled by your carrier.
Looks like your mobile data connection is disabled in settings. Enable it to get back online.
Your mobile data connection has been overridden and is disabled now.
Looks like your mobile data is disabled because it reached monthly quota. Please increase the quota to get back online.
Looks like your device has roaming data disabled. Turn it on to get back online
Your phone is too hot. Please switch it off to cool down and try again.
Your mobile data connection is disabled for an unknown reason.
Your mobile data connection is disabled in settings. Enable it to get back online.
Your mobile network is experiencing congestion, which may affect your internet experience.
Your phone has no mobile data connection.
You have a poor 3G mobile signal which may affect your internet experience.
You have a poor 4G mobile signal which may affect your internet experience.
You have a poor 5G mobile signal which may affect your internet experience.
It looks like your WiFi signal is weak. You are currently using a 5GHz WiFi connection, but you may try connecting to a 2.4GHz WiFi connection if available.
You have a poor WiFi signal which may affect your internet experience.
Your power save mode is ON, which may affect your internet experience.
There is a problem with your internet connection.
Looks like your mobile data connection is slow. Connect to WiFi or try getting a better signal.
Your network connection appears to be slow. This could be because the network is busy (congestion).
Looks like power save mode is enabled. It may prevent app notifications and downloading images or email attachments. Please disable it.
Looks like your LTE is slow. Please connect to WiFi or use the compass to find a better signal.
Your roaming network connection appears to be slow.
Looks like your WiFi is slow. Please connect to a different network.
It usually takes a few seconds.
You are connected to the internet.
Become a super user and help us build Coverage & Cell Tower maps! Learn how
To compare operators\' speeds and coverage in your area and make the most of the feature, please grant location permission in the settings.
This dialog will self destruct in...um, whenever you click CANCEL. You won\'t see it again.
Be the first to see the new prototype and tell us what do you think about it! Your feedback might change this app!
The cell tower direction/arrow is supported\n for 2G/3G and 4G/LTE at the moment.
Tell us your problem by emailing us directly at bugs@opensignal.com
This action will close the app

That\'s what we\'re building. A data source that shows how users experience mobile networks worldwide. Yes, it\'s possible!

To do this we collect data on the signal strength, network, location and other device sensors. You can stop this at any time in the settings.

We share this data with network operators globally and others in the industry to help understand and improve connectivity and application experiences on wireless networks. We also collect data on how you use this app and crash reports so we can improve it.
Click OK to agree to this, for more see our privacy policy. By clicking OK below you confirm you are 18 years of age or older.
We show how people experience mobile networks worldwide
Total time when video stopped playing.
Time it takes for the video to start playing.
Percent of test time that video is playing and not loading or buffering.
The test will randomly select SD or HD video to give you a full picture of your experience.
Help us improve Opensignal: email us your feature ideas, or join the community to become a beta tester.
A fast ImageView (and Drawable) that supports rounded corners (and ovals or circles) based on the original example from Romain Guy.
So you can use and contribute to the coverage map and compare speeds and signal of operators in your area.
Location settings are inadequate, and cannot be fixed here. Fix in Settings.
Date does not match expected pattern: %1$s
Date out of expected year range %1$s - %2$s
Swipe to select a year, or tap to switch back to selecting a day
Scroll to show the next month
Scroll to show the previous month
Switch to text input mode for the time input
Switch to clock mode for the time input
Our maps are built by our users. No estimates, just billions of measurements from users like you.
Our maps are 100% based on data contributed by users. Gaps on the map mean we don\'t have data in that area.
Strong means above -85dBm, you\'ll get good data speeds & call quality
Weak means below -99dBm, you might get poor data speeds & dropped calls
Switch to clock mode for the time input.
Switch to text input mode for the time input.
Some features may not work properly when missing the location permission.
Some features may not work properly when missing the phone call permission.
Start date selection: %1$s – End date selection: %2$s

There are 2 types of operators - those that own their own infrastructure of towers (\"host operators\") and those which rent infrastructure (\"virtual operators\"). Opensignal only shows the host operators, so if you are on a virtual operator, you need to find out the host operator to use the maps and Network Stats.
The Network Stats shows how users in the area on the map experienced their network on average over a 60-day period.

A latency test is a very quick test which measures how fast a tiny amount of data can be sent from your phone to the internet and back again. A low latency is good for voice or video calls over the internet - for example Skype or Apple\'s FaceTime.
Download speed is how fast data can be downloaded to your device. The result is measured in megabits per second (Mbps). A high download speed is good for fast web browsing and streaming video.

Our upload test measures how fast data can be uploaded from your device to the internet. The result is measured in megabits per second (MBps). A high upload speed is good for fast sharing of photos or videos to social networks like Facebook or Twitter.
This shows the average speeds users experienced in this area.
2G and 3G are older technologies. These older networks typically have a large geographic coverage, but deliver slower data speeds.
4G is a newer generation of network technology. 4G (sometimes called \"LTE\") provides fast data speeds suitable for streaming video, and voice calls with very good audio quality.

You can view the maps by different network types. The \"G\" in networks refers to the \"generation\" of the underlying wireless networks technology. 2G and 3G are older, slower technologies. 4G gives higher data speeds. 5G is the most recent and fastest technology, with lower latency and increased speeds compared to 4G. Whilst Opensignal speed and video tests work with all connections, including 5G, the coverage map includes network coverage information up to 4G at the moment.
Networks Stats not available. Please change zoom, location, or filters.

We\'ve had millions of downloads worldwide, and our users contribute billions of signal readings and speed tests every day. However, our users haven\'t contributed data on their networks everywhere. If your operator is not listed, try changing the zoom or moving the map. Help us collect the most accurate mobile network stats by using the app to contribute data about your experience.
Network Stats is unavailable at this zoom level. Please zoom out.
Network Stats is unavailable at this zoom level. Please zoom in.
We also measure how much data your apps consume.
We will ask you for a couple of permissions to let this app work
There are a few reasons why your operator might not be in Network Stats.
We read your signal status and detect dual sim cards. We don\'t make calls.
Let us know what you don\'t like about the app
How would you rate your experience using the app?
Refreshing cache\nNext time will be faster.
Select at least one network type

Opensignal helps you measure the actual experience you receive on mobile networks, see the experience of users on all the networks in your area, and compare your results with everyone\'s results.

Opensignal\'s measurements come from millions of users all over the world. We collect billions of measurements every day. So, you\'re in good company.

If you have comments or suggestions for the app, just say hi@opensignal.com - we\'d love to hear from you.
Opensignal measures connectivity information to help operators improve their networks. To learn more about how we use your data see our privacy policy.
Get notified when your signal goes below this level (a higher threshold means more notifications):
An internal error occurred: please check your internet connection and retry.
Availability is the measure of how much time you\'ve been connected to a given network. It is calculated using measurements of your network signal.
It can take some time for stats to become available. Check back later!
Still looking for towers. Try again when you see them on map

Help us to collect and share with other users like you the most accurate coverage data, cell tower location and mobile network stats.

You can do this by enabling background location permissions in the settings.

We’ve had millions of downloads worldwide, and our users contribute in signal readings and speed tests. However, to keep the map up to date we need to collect measurements every day.

To become a super user, please allow readings all the time.
Our Coverage & Cell Tower maps\n are built by the crowd.
There is no application to open this link
Please enter a valid email address.
You\'ve reached the limit of available answer edits.

SUTEL and Opensignal have partnered to measure the user experience of Costa Rica\'s networks and find new opportunities for improvement.
To do this we collect data on the signal strength, network, location and other device sensors. You can stop this at any time in the settings.

We share this data with SUTEL, network operators globally and others in the industry to help understand and improve connectivity and application experiences on wireless networks. We also collect data on how you use this app and crash reports so we can improve it.

Click OK to agree to this, for more see our privacy policy. By clicking OK below you confirm you are 18 years of age or older.
Let\'s measure Costa Rica\'s networks, together
I\'m in! Take me to the settings
Sorry, there was a problem connecting
View our full terms of service on our website https://www.opensignal.com/terms-conditions
Thanks for helping us collect measurements!
We\'d love to hear your thoughts.
Please zoom in to see towers.
Please zoom out to see towers.
